statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 23 of 27 countries holding critical levels in at least one fuel type, while oil prices remain elevated at $93.09 per barrel.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure across multiple fuel types, with widespread shortfalls below the mandatory 90-day minimum for net imports. Of the 27 EU countries analyzed, 23 nations hold critical levels in at least one fuel category, indicating systemic stress across the bloc's energy infrastructure.

Diesel supplies are particularly concerning, with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves below 90 days, averaging just 88.8 days EU-wide. Several major economies face severe diesel shortages: Romania (30.2 days), Slovakia (44.7 days), and Austria (55.2 days). Petrol reserves fare slightly better at 96.3 days average, but 17 countries remain in critical territory. Luxembourg faces the most severe overall shortage with all three fuel types in critical status.

Jet fuel presents a mixed picture with an 87.3-day EU average, but stark regional variations. While some countries like Slovenia (365 days) and Estonia (320 days) maintain substantial reserves, 19 of 27 nations hold critical jet fuel stocks. The aviation fuel shortage is particularly acute in Poland (22.5 days) and Portugal (21.2 days).

The elevated Brent crude price of $93.09 per barrel, up 28.4% since late February's Middle East escalation, compounds these supply pressures. This price level, coupled with widespread strategic reserve releases, suggests ongoing market tensions that could further strain European fuel security if supply disruptions persist.
keyPoints: 23 of 27 EU countries hold critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with diesel most affected (19 countries below 90-day minimum)
Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, and Romania face the most severe shortages across multiple fuel types
Jet fuel supplies show extreme regional variation, from Slovenia's 365-day reserves to Poland's 22.5-day shortage
Oil prices remain elevated at $93.09 per barrel, up 28.4% since February's Middle East escalation
Recent strategic reserve releases indicate countries are already drawing on emergency stocks to maintain supply
